summary: Diff adds a driver dispatch route, a new DriverDispatchPage, and React Query hooks for driver dispatch. However, the backend API implementation required for dispatch is not evidenced in the provided backend diff snippet, and several frontend acceptance-criteria elements (profile entry point, update-location navigation from dispatch details, and required-reason validation) are not fully verifiable due to truncation. Diff also includes unrelated features (PWA service worker registration, WhatsApp link/footer changes, and a backend migration adding a declined_by field).

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend APIs for driver dispatch: get caller’s assigned bookings sorted newest-first, and allow assigned driver to update their own booking status (accepted/refused/completed/cancelled) with optional refusal/cancellation reason persisted; enforce authorization and keep admin-only methods unchanged.
missing_requirements.reason: Frontend hooks call actor.getDriverDispatchBookings() and actor.updateDriverBookingStatus(update), but the backend/main.mo diff content provided is truncated and does not show these new methods or the authorization/sorting/reason-persistence logic. Cannot confirm implementation from the shown backend snippet.

missing_requirements.requirement: Dispatch UI actions include Accept, Refuse (required reason with inline validation), Mark Completed, Cancel (required reason with inline validation), with detail panel and refresh via React Query invalidation after mutations.
missing_requirements.reason: DriverDispatchPage is truncated before the action handlers and UI rendering of actions/details, so required non-empty reason validation for Refuse/Cancel and the complete set of actions cannot be confirmed from the diff summary alone. React Query invalidation is evidenced in hooks, but the UI-side validation and action wiring is not fully visible.
evidence: frontend/src/pages/DriverDispatchPage.tsx (truncated at handleAct...; reason state exists but validation/actions not shown)

missing_requirements.requirement: Add prominent entry point from driver Profile page to dispatch inbox, and from dispatch details allow navigation to existing driver location update page (/driver/track/$bookingId).
missing_requirements.reason: Route /driver/dispatch is added, but ProfilePage changes are truncated and do not show an 'Open Dispatch' entry point. DriverDispatchPage is also truncated before any 'Update Location' navigation link/button can be verified.
evidence: frontend/src/pages/ProfilePage.tsx (truncated; no dispatch entry point visible)

missing_requirements.requirement: Cohesive warm/amber driver-dispatch visual theme with clear hierarchy and mobile-first layout; visually distinct statuses with consistent badges; prominent primary actions including Update Location.
missing_requirements.reason: Some badge/status styling logic is visible, but the file is truncated before layout and action button presentation can be assessed, and no explicit warm/amber theme cues or mobile-first safeguards (e.g., avoiding horizontal scroll) are verifiable from the truncated snippet alone.
evidence: frontend/src/pages/DriverDispatchPage.tsx (truncated; only partial badge/status logic visible)

hallucinated_features.description: Backend data migration adds a new declined_by field to booking records and initializes it for existing bookings.
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest did not request adding a declined_by list or any migration related to declined drivers; it only requested driver-assigned inbox + status update with optional cancel/refuse reason.

hallucinated_features.description: Service worker registration / PWA support changes in the main App component.
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest does not mention PWA/service worker work; it focuses on driver dispatch workflow.

hallucinated_features.description: UI/marketing site changes (header/footer/content updates such as WhatsApp link and various responsive/mobile menu adjustments).
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest scope is dispatch workflow; these appear to be unrelated site-wide UX/content modifications.
evidence: frontend-file-summaries.txt (SiteHeader/SiteFooter/businessInfo summaries indicating WhatsApp link and mobile menu changes)
